strongestIdea: Add one tiny thumb-polished brass elevator-call button token on Otis's short vest watch chain, used as a subtle concierge habit when he listens, thinks, or welcomes the user.
uniquenessClaim: This lane contributes one controlled odd-but-lovable specificity with a hard ship-ready rejection rule, rather than broad identity, silhouette, material, lighting, or comedy direction. The idea is memorable but intentionally small enough to remove if it becomes too weird.
qualityRisks: No local generated image files were created, so the specificity is prompt-proven only.
The parent source-resolution and alpha/chroma blockers remain unresolved for actual production sprites.
The token may become too large, shiny, magical, jewelry-like, or noisy without strict negative prompting.
Hand, chain, and token interactions can create malformed fingers or mask-hostile overlaps.
Formal outfit transfer may become cluttered unless the token is mostly hidden.
fallbackReason: Requested GPT-5.5 fast mode with extra-high reasoning was not exposed in this environment; used the available Codex model.
promotionBlockers: No native 4K transparent source images exist from this lane.
No image preflight was run because this lane produced prompt and QA artifacts only.
No staged derivatives, contact sheet, app-scale preview, or CharacterStage proof exists.
Coordinator must generate probes, enforce the weirdness rejection rubric, run source and app-scale QA, and obtain the exact approved for app phrase before any promotion.
